title: Double-Crisp Fried Irish Kale Hash
description: A crispy, savory Irish dish featuring kale, cabbage, and Kabse spice, perfect for meal prep.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) and a lazy Susan spinner to medium-high heat.
Remove the leaves from the kale and cabbage bunches and discard the stems.
In a large bowl, massage the kale and cabbage leaves with your hands for 5-7 minutes to soften them.
Add the minced garlic, Kabse spice blend, salt, and black pepper to the bowl and massage for another 2-3 minutes.
Heat 2 tbsp of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and add 1/2 of the kale and cabbage mixture.
Fry the mixture for 3-4 minutes on each side, until crispy and golden brown.
Transfer the fried mixture to the lazy Susan spinner and repeat the process with the remaining kale and cabbage mixture.
Drain excess oil from the spinner and serve the Double-Crisp Fried Irish Kale Hash hot, garnished with tempering chocolate chips if desired.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
